title: Cluster domain auto-detection returns the wrong domain for namespaces sorting before "cluster"
body:
## Expected Behavior

When `KubeClusterDomain` is not set explicitly, the injector auto-detects it
from `/etc/resolv.conf`. It should detect the actual cluster domain (e.g.
`cluster.local`) regardless of the pod's namespace.

## Actual Behavior

`utils.getClusterDomain` selects the cluster domain by sorting the resolv.conf
`search` domains alphabetically and taking the first one:

```go
searchDomains := getResolvSearchDomains(resolvConf)
sort.Strings(searchDomains)
... kubeClusterDomain = searchDomains[0]
```

In a Kubernetes pod the search list is
`<namespace>.svc.<cluster-domain> svc.<cluster-domain> <cluster-domain>`.
Alphabetical sorting only yields the cluster domain when the namespace sorts
after `cluster`. For a namespace such as `app`, the search list is:

```
search app.svc.cluster.local svc.cluster.local cluster.local
```

and detection returns `app.svc.cluster.local` instead of `cluster.local`.

This feeds `pkg/injector/service/config.go` (`GetKubeClusterDomain()`), so
sidecars injected in affected namespaces can be configured with the wrong
cluster domain.

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

Call `getClusterDomain` with a resolv.conf whose namespace sorts before
`cluster`:

```
search app.svc.cluster.local svc.cluster.local cluster.local
```

Observed result: `app.svc.cluster.local`. Expected: `cluster.local`.

## Release Note

RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Cluster domain auto-detection for namespaces that sort alphabetically before "cluster".

comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed suggestion — that's more robust than my initial length-based heuristic, so I've updated the PR to use it.

Detection now finds the search entry that begins with the `svc.` label and strips it, e.g. `svc.cluster.local` → `cluster.local`. The namespace-qualified entry (`<namespace>.svc.<cluster-domain>`) does not start with `svc.`, so it isn't matched. This preserves the resolver's search order, doesn't assume a fixed number of namespace labels, and returns the default cluster domain explicitly when no `svc.`-shaped entry exists.

Table tests were expanded accordingly: namespaces `app` and `zoo` (either side of "cluster"), multi-label namespaces, a custom multi-label cluster domain (`svc.k8s.corp.example` → `k8s.corp.example`), trailing dots on the search entries, unrelated search entries mixed in, and the no-match fallback.
